categories.1: governed mutation routed through the orchestrator
categories.3: generated public-output mutation outside governed records
categories.6: unacceptable bypass requiring removal or refactoring

assuranceStatement: AST/import analysis inventories discovered direct and transitive mutation capabilities; unknown or unowned capability fails closed. This is an enforceable inventory, not a claim that textual search proves absence of every bypass.

classified.justification: Constitution guardian reads governed records and writes only a generated report file (docs/agent-reports/).

Approved validator execution boundary: constructs a terminable worker_threads.Worker per validator with a hard timeout and reconstructs a serializable context. No governed mutation; the sole worker-execution site for validators.
Phase 3 execution infrastructure that durably records validation artifacts bound to committed ledger transitions.
Validation result normalization within the execution layer.

Validator execution worker: executes ONLY verified closure bytes via an in-memory module system (vm.compileFunction, static builtin allowlist, no path-require fallback). Reads each closure module through a single O_NOFOLLOW read-only descriptor (fstat+read+hash on the same fd) and verifies the bound manifest (hash/size/mode/uid/gid/dev/ino/nlink) before executing; the read-only fs.open* primitives are modeled by the audit as filesystem/durable capabilities though no governed state is mutated.
Action-specific ADS semantic validator.

Internal source-boundary validator registry core that statically inspects verified module bytes, builds reviewed closures, and exposes immutable descriptor lookups.
